{{nihongo|'''Murayama Shrine'''|村山神社 (亀岡市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tanba Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 村山神社 |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
本殿（右奥）と八幡宮（左手前） |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=村山神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
{{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=村山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''村山神社'''（むらやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の通り。 ; 主祭神（本殿） :* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}（おおやまづみのみこと） :* {{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}}（このはなさくやひめのみこと） ; 配祀神（八幡宮） :* {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} === 歴史 === 本殿・八幡宮とも創建は不詳。境内背後の台地上には須恵器等の窯跡が見つかっており、裏山からもその破片が確認されている。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では丹波国{{jalink|桑田郡|Q11537600}}に「村山神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 社伝では、古くは広大な社地を有していたが兵乱で焼失し、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}27年（{{jalink|1420年|Q6209}}）に国恩寺の雑掌として当地を治めた渡辺六郎頼方が社殿を再興、その頃八幡宮を勧請して並立したという『京都府の地名』村山神社項。。 {{jalink|2017年|Q25290}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}29年）には、{{jalink|平成29年台風第21号|Q42108577|台風21号}}で八幡宮本殿が倒壊する被害に遭っている[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "台風被害の未指定文化財修理に京都府が12件・483万円補助"]（産経新聞、2018年2月14日記事）。。 === 境内 === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|本殿（右）と八幡宮（左）
{{small|最上段の板垣内に同一の規模で鎮座。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|参道（拝殿から上段方向）
{{small|本殿・八幡宮それぞれに参道を付す。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|境内裏山
{{small|祭神が降臨したといわれる禁足地。}} === 摂末社 === * 百太夫社 * 厳島社 * 広田社 * 天皇社 - 祭神：{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}} * 五社大明神 - 稲荷社・天満宮社・大国主命社・野々社・愛宕社 * 稲荷社 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭 （{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭 （{{jalink|春分の日|Q1049141}}） * 八幡宮例祭 （{{jalink|8月20日|Q2824}}） * 例祭 （{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}） * 新嘗祭 （{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125}}（JR西日本）{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}} {{jalink|馬堀駅|Q1044103}} （徒歩約20分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）亀岡市 村山神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]]

Commons category (P373) == * Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府亀岡市篠町森山先34', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:村山神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 村山神社 |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
本殿（右奥）と八幡宮（左手前） |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=村山神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
{{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=村山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''村山神社'''（むらやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の通り。 ; 主祭神（本殿） :* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}（おおやまづみのみこと） :* {{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}}（このはなさくやひめのみこと） ; 配祀神（八幡宮） :* {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} === 歴史 === 本殿・八幡宮とも創建は不詳。境内背後の台地上には須恵器等の窯跡が見つかっており、裏山からもその破片が確認されている。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では丹波国{{jalink|桑田郡|Q11537600}}に「村山神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 社伝では、古くは広大な社地を有していたが兵乱で焼失し、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}27年（{{jalink|1420年|Q6209}}）に国恩寺の雑掌として当地を治めた渡辺六郎頼方が社殿を再興、その頃八幡宮を勧請して並立したという『京都府の地名』村山神社項。。 {{jalink|2017年|Q25290}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}29年）には、{{jalink|平成29年台風第21号|Q42108577|台風21号}}で八幡宮本殿が倒壊する被害に遭っている[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "台風被害の未指定文化財修理に京都府が12件・483万円補助"]（産経新聞、2018年2月14日記事）。。 === 境内 === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|本殿（右）と八幡宮（左）
{{small|最上段の板垣内に同一の規模で鎮座。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|参道（拝殿から上段方向）
{{small|本殿・八幡宮それぞれに参道を付す。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|境内裏山
{{small|祭神が降臨したといわれる禁足地。}} === 摂末社 === * 百太夫社 * 厳島社 * 広田社 * 天皇社 - 祭神：{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}} * 五社大明神 - 稲荷社・天満宮社・大国主命社・野々社・愛宕社 * 稲荷社 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭 （{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭 （{{jalink|春分の日|Q1049141}}） * 八幡宮例祭 （{{jalink|8月20日|Q2824}}） * 例祭 （{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}） * 新嘗祭 （{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125}}（JR西日本）{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}} {{jalink|馬堀駅|Q1044103}} （徒歩約20分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）亀岡市 村山神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]]

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 村山神社 |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
本殿（右奥）と八幡宮（左手前） |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=村山神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
{{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=村山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''村山神社'''（むらやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の通り。 ; 主祭神（本殿） :* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}（おおやまづみのみこと） :* {{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}}（このはなさくやひめのみこと） ; 配祀神（八幡宮） :* {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} === 歴史 === 本殿・八幡宮とも創建は不詳。境内背後の台地上には須恵器等の窯跡が見つかっており、裏山からもその破片が確認されている。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では丹波国{{jalink|桑田郡|Q11537600}}に「村山神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 社伝では、古くは広大な社地を有していたが兵乱で焼失し、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}27年（{{jalink|1420年|Q6209}}）に国恩寺の雑掌として当地を治めた渡辺六郎頼方が社殿を再興、その頃八幡宮を勧請して並立したという『京都府の地名』村山神社項。。 {{jalink|2017年|Q25290}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}29年）には、{{jalink|平成29年台風第21号|Q42108577|台風21号}}で八幡宮本殿が倒壊する被害に遭っている[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "台風被害の未指定文化財修理に京都府が12件・483万円補助"]（産経新聞、2018年2月14日記事）。。 === 境内 === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|本殿（右）と八幡宮（左）
{{small|最上段の板垣内に同一の規模で鎮座。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|参道（拝殿から上段方向）
{{small|本殿・八幡宮それぞれに参道を付す。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|境内裏山
{{small|祭神が降臨したといわれる禁足地。}} === 摂末社 === * 百太夫社 * 厳島社 * 広田社 * 天皇社 - 祭神：{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}} * 五社大明神 - 稲荷社・天満宮社・大国主命社・野々社・愛宕社 * 稲荷社 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭 （{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭 （{{jalink|春分の日|Q1049141}}） * 八幡宮例祭 （{{jalink|8月20日|Q2824}}） * 例祭 （{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}） * 新嘗祭 （{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125}}（JR西日本）{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}} {{jalink|馬堀駅|Q1044103}} （徒歩約20分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）亀岡市 村山神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{small|最上段の板垣内に同一の規模で鎮座。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|参道（拝殿から上段方向）
{{small|本殿・八幡宮それぞれに参道を付す。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|境内裏山

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 村山神社 |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
本殿（右奥）と八幡宮（左手前） |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=村山神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
{{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=村山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''村山神社'''（むらやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の通り。 ; 主祭神（本殿） :* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}（おおやまづみのみこと） :* {{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}}（このはなさくやひめのみこと） ; 配祀神（八幡宮） :* {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} === 歴史 === 本殿・八幡宮とも創建は不詳。境内背後の台地上には須恵器等の窯跡が見つかっており、裏山からもその破片が確認されている。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では丹波国{{jalink|桑田郡|Q11537600}}に「村山神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 社伝では、古くは広大な社地を有していたが兵乱で焼失し、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}27年（{{jalink|1420年|Q6209}}）に国恩寺の雑掌として当地を治めた渡辺六郎頼方が社殿を再興、その頃八幡宮を勧請して並立したという『京都府の地名』村山神社項。。 {{jalink|2017年|Q25290}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}29年）には、{{jalink|平成29年台風第21号|Q42108577|台風21号}}で八幡宮本殿が倒壊する被害に遭っている[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "台風被害の未指定文化財修理に京都府が12件・483万円補助"]（産経新聞、2018年2月14日記事）。。 === 境内 === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|本殿（右）と八幡宮（左）
{{small|最上段の板垣内に同一の規模で鎮座。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|参道（拝殿から上段方向）
{{small|本殿・八幡宮それぞれに参道を付す。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|境内裏山
{{small|祭神が降臨したといわれる禁足地。}} === 摂末社 === * 百太夫社 * 厳島社 * 広田社 * 天皇社 - 祭神：{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}} * 五社大明神 - 稲荷社・天満宮社・大国主命社・野々社・愛宕社 * 稲荷社 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭 （{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭 （{{jalink|春分の日|Q1049141}}） * 八幡宮例祭 （{{jalink|8月20日|Q2824}}） * 例祭 （{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}） * 新嘗祭 （{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125}}（JR西日本）{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}} {{jalink|馬堀駅|Q1044103}} （徒歩約20分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）亀岡市 村山神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}

Commons category (P373) == * Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府亀岡市篠町森山先34', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:村山神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 村山神社 |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
本殿（右奥）と八幡宮（左手前） |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=村山神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
{{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=村山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''村山神社'''（むらやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の通り。 ; 主祭神（本殿） :* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}（おおやまづみのみこと） :* {{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}}（このはなさくやひめのみこと） ; 配祀神（八幡宮） :* {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} === 歴史 === 本殿・八幡宮とも創建は不詳。境内背後の台地上には須恵器等の窯跡が見つかっており、裏山からもその破片が確認されている。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では丹波国{{jalink|桑田郡|Q11537600}}に「村山神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 社伝では、古くは広大な社地を有していたが兵乱で焼失し、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}27年（{{jalink|1420年|Q6209}}）に国恩寺の雑掌として当地を治めた渡辺六郎頼方が社殿を再興、その頃八幡宮を勧請して並立したという『京都府の地名』村山神社項。。 {{jalink|2017年|Q25290}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}29年）には、{{jalink|平成29年台風第21号|Q42108577|台風21号}}で八幡宮本殿が倒壊する被害に遭っている[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "台風被害の未指定文化財修理に京都府が12件・483万円補助"]（産経新聞、2018年2月14日記事）。。 === 境内 === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|本殿（右）と八幡宮（左）
{{small|最上段の板垣内に同一の規模で鎮座。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|参道（拝殿から上段方向）
{{small|本殿・八幡宮それぞれに参道を付す。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|境内裏山
{{small|祭神が降臨したといわれる禁足地。}} === 摂末社 === * 百太夫社 * 厳島社 * 広田社 * 天皇社 - 祭神：{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}} * 五社大明神 - 稲荷社・天満宮社・大国主命社・野々社・愛宕社 * 稲荷社 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭 （{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭 （{{jalink|春分の日|Q1049141}}） * 八幡宮例祭 （{{jalink|8月20日|Q2824}}） * 例祭 （{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}） * 新嘗祭 （{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125}}（JR西日本）{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}} {{jalink|馬堀駅|Q1044103}} （徒歩約20分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）亀岡市 村山神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 村山神社 |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
本殿（右奥）と八幡宮（左手前） |所在地 = {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=村山神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}
{{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=村山神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''村山神社'''（むらやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の通り。 ; 主祭神（本殿） :* {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}}（おおやまづみのみこと） :* {{jalink|コノハナサクヤヒメ||木花咲耶比売命}}（このはなさくやひめのみこと） ; 配祀神（八幡宮） :* {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119}} === 歴史 === 本殿・八幡宮とも創建は不詳。境内背後の台地上には須恵器等の窯跡が見つかっており、裏山からもその破片が確認されている。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では丹波国{{jalink|桑田郡|Q11537600}}に「村山神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 社伝では、古くは広大な社地を有していたが兵乱で焼失し、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}27年（{{jalink|1420年|Q6209}}）に国恩寺の雑掌として当地を治めた渡辺六郎頼方が社殿を再興、その頃八幡宮を勧請して並立したという『京都府の地名』村山神社項。。 {{jalink|2017年|Q25290}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}29年）には、{{jalink|平成29年台風第21号|Q42108577|台風21号}}で八幡宮本殿が倒壊する被害に遭っている[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "台風被害の未指定文化財修理に京都府が12件・483万円補助"]（産経新聞、2018年2月14日記事）。。 === 境内 === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|本殿（右）と八幡宮（左）
{{small|最上段の板垣内に同一の規模で鎮座。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|参道（拝殿から上段方向）
{{small|本殿・八幡宮それぞれに参道を付す。}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|境内裏山
{{small|祭神が降臨したといわれる禁足地。}} === 摂末社 === * 百太夫社 * 厳島社 * 広田社 * 天皇社 - 祭神：{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}} * 五社大明神 - 稲荷社・天満宮社・大国主命社・野々社・愛宕社 * 稲荷社 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭 （{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭 （{{jalink|春分の日|Q1049141}}） * 八幡宮例祭 （{{jalink|8月20日|Q2824}}） * 例祭 （{{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}}） * 新嘗祭 （{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|京都府|Q120730}}{{jalink|亀岡市|Q633996}}篠町森山先34 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125}}（JR西日本）{{jalink|嵯峨野線|Q1073011}} {{jalink|馬堀駅|Q1044103}} （徒歩約20分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）亀岡市 村山神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}

former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime||Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}} of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' completed in {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Kuwata District|Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 27 ({{jalink|1420|Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{jalink|2017|Q25290}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{jalink|Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{jalink|Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{jalink|August 20|Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime||Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime||Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}} of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' completed in {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Kuwata District|Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 27 ({{jalink|1420|Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{jalink|2017|Q25290}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{jalink|Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{jalink|Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{jalink|August 20|Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime||Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime||Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}} of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' completed in {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Kuwata District|Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 27 ({{jalink|1420|Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{jalink|2017|Q25290}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{jalink|Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{jalink|Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{jalink|August 20|Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime||Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}, {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime||Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} :* {{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}} of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' completed in {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Kuwata District|Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} 27 ({{jalink|1420|Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{jalink|2017|Q25290}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{jalink|Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{jalink|Emperor Chūai|Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{jalink|Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{jalink|August 20|Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{jalink|October 25|Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730}}{{jalink|Kameoka|Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} {{jalink|Sagano Line|Q1073011}} {{jalink|Umahori Station|Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime||WD=|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime||WD=|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime||WD=|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:村山神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
