category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:村山神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むらやましんしやかめおかし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMurayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)}}[[Category:qq]]

category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:村山神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]] [[Category:qq]]

Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)

Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]]

Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)

Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|WD=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|WD=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|WD=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|WD=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|WD=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:村山神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|qid=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|qid=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|qid=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|qid=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|qid=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|qid=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|qid=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|qid=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|qid=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|qid=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|qid=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

category|Category:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:村山神社 (亀岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11523148}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Murayama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Honden (rear right) and Hachiman-gū (front left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|59|59.45|N|135|35|53.89|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Murayama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|qid=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|qid=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|qid=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|qid=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|qid=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
{{small|Both enshrined at the top level inside board fence with same scale.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) sandou.JPG|Approach (from haiden to upper level)
{{small|Separate approaches for honden and Hachiman-gū.}} File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) urayama.JPG|Back mountain of precinct
{{small|Restricted sacred area where deity is said to have descended.}} === Sessha and massha === * Hyakudayū-sha * Itsukushima-sha * Hirota-sha * Tennō-sha - deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Gosha Daimyōjin - Inari-sha, Tenman-gū-sha, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto-sha, Nono-sha, Atago-sha * Inari-sha === Festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|Q1049141|qid=Q1049141|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * Hachiman-gū annual festival ({{ill|lt=August 20|Q2824|qid=Q2824|1=August 20}}) * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|qid=Q2954|1=October 25}}) * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|qid=Q3021|1=November 23}}) === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Shino-chō Mori Yamasaki 34 '''Access''' * Rail: {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|qid=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Umahori Station|Q1044103|qid=Q1044103|1=Umahori Station}} (about 20 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Murayama-jinja (Kameoka)}} * Shrine origin text * 'Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Murayama Shrine section {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Murayama Shrine (Q11523148)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|qid=Q2954|1=October 25}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Murayama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Murayama Shrine''' (むらやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Mori, Shino-chō, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are as follows. ; Main deities (honden) :* {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまづみのみこと) :* {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) ; Associated deities (Hachiman-gū) :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} :* {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === History === Founding is unknown for both honden and Hachiman-gū. Kiln remains such as Sue ware have been found on the plateau behind the precinct, and shards are also confirmed from the back mountain. In the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), "Murayama Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|qid=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}} of Tanba Province, and ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Shrine tradition says it once had vast shrine lands but burned in warfare; in {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|qid=Q6209|1=1420}}), Watanabe Rokurō Yorikata, administrator of Kokuon-ji who governed this area, restored shrine buildings, and around that time invited Hachiman-gū to stand alongside'Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture,' Murayama Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|qid=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 29), Hachiman-gū honden collapsed due to {{ill|lt=Typhoon 21 (2017)|Q42108577|qid=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon 21|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20180214-MTZP3UOMHFNCNGABYEAKXISHMA/ "Kyoto Prefecture subsidizes 12 undesignated cultural property repairs for typhoon damage, 4.83 million yen"] (Sankei Shimbun, Feb 14, 2018).. === Precinct === File:Murayama-jinja (Kameoka) shaden-2.JPG|Honden (right) and Hachiman-gū (left)
